papers.title: Engaging the head and the heart: why scientists turn to poetry
papers.authors: Josie Glausiusz
papers.summary: Researchers use poetry to visualize complex problems and communicate science to a wider audience.

papers.title: ‘Yes, we can’: a blueprint for a clean economy and healthy society
papers.authors: Andrew Macintosh
papers.summary: An economist proposes a path to a more efficient, prosperous and sustainable world by transitioning to a clean economy.

papers.title: Why the US needs a unified, mission-based strategy for health innovation
papers.authors: Victor J. Dzau, Melissa H. Laitner, Emily L. Shambaugh, Alex M. Helman
papers.summary: A unified, mission-based strategy for health innovation is needed in the US to address unmet medical needs and improve treatment reach.

papers.title: Moon fly-by live coverage: Artemis crew see intriguing colours on lunar surface
papers.authors: Alexandra Witze
papers.summary: Astronauts on the Artemis mission observe intriguing colors on the lunar surface during a fly-by of the Moon's far side.

papers.title: What Artemis II’s astronauts will look for on the Moon’s far side
papers.authors: Alexandra Witze
papers.summary: Mission scientists discuss the geological features they're most excited for the Artemis II crew to observe on the Moon's far side.

papers.title: Massive budget cuts for US science proposed again by Trump administration
papers.authors: Max Kozlov, Dan Garisto, Edward Chen
papers.summary: The Trump administration has proposed significant budget cuts for US science, which would also impact federal funding for scientific publishing.

papers.title: Regular physical activity in midlife cuts risk of early death
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Regular physical activity in midlife has been shown to significantly reduce the risk of early death, with even a few hours of moderate-to-vigorous exercise per week having a strong effect.

papers.title: Artemis II is go: humans head to the Moon after half-century absence
papers.authors: Benjamin Thompson, Alexandra Witze
papers.summary: The Artemis II mission aims to send humans back to the Moon after a half-century absence, with various scientific experiments planned for the journey.

papers.title: The Moon belongs to all of us — not just countries that can afford to reach it
papers.authors: Moriba Jah
papers.summary: The author argues that the Moon should be considered a shared resource for humanity, rather than being controlled by countries with the means to reach it.

papers.title: ‘Treasure trove’ of antiviral proteins could inspire powerful molecular tools
papers.authors: Miryam Naddaf
papers.summary: Researchers have identified a 'treasure trove' of antiviral proteins in bacteria, which could inspire the development of powerful molecular tools.

papers.title: How procrastination can rob you of career fulfilment in science
papers.authors: Holly Newson
papers.summary: This paper discusses how procrastination can negatively impact career fulfillment in science.

papers.title: ‘It’s a real shock’: quantum-computing breakthroughs pose imminent risks to cybersecurity
papers.authors: Davide Castelvecchi
papers.summary: Quantum computing breakthroughs pose a risk to cybersecurity, potentially compromising security keys and cryptocurrencies.

papers.title: China is planning to land people on the Moon — and might beat the United States to it
papers.authors: Jonathan O’Callaghan
papers.summary: China plans to land people on the Moon by 2030 and establish a permanent lunar base, potentially beating the US to it.

papers.title: Mix-and-match synthesis of 3D small molecules
papers.authors: Huyen N. Nguyen, Daniel J. Blair
papers.summary: A new method for synthesizing 3D small molecules has been developed, enabling modular synthesis and potential applications in drug discovery and materials design.

papers.title: Breakthrough computer chip tech could help meet ‘monumental demand’ driven by AI
papers.authors: Katherine Bourzac
papers.summary: A new computer chip technology uses giant mirrors to produce tiny transistors and circuits, potentially meeting the demand driven by AI.

papers.title: Why some cancer-fighting immune cells lose their strength inside tumours
papers.authors: Edward Chen
papers.summary: Researchers found that some cancer-fighting immune cells called dendritic cells lose their strength when inside tumors due to a lack of mitochondria.

papers.title: Structural modifications in strain-engineered bilayer nickelate thin films
papers.authors: Lopa Bhatt, Edgar Abarca Morales, Abigail Y. Jiang, Eun Kyo Ko, Yi-Feng Zhao et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: Scientists engineered bilayer nickelate thin films and found structural modifications that can be made to improve their properties.

papers.title: These scientists chased a jet to learn more about ‘lean-burn’ contrails
papers.authors: Benjamin Thompson, Shamini Bundell
papers.summary: Researchers conducted an in-flight experiment to study contrails and found that reducing soot emissions may not necessarily reduce their formation.

papers.title: These advanced solar cells have an antique source: old bullets
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Scientists discovered that old bullets can be recycled to provide raw materials for advanced solar cells.

papers.title: DNA damage burden causes selective CUX2 neuron loss in neuroinflammation
papers.authors: Laura Morcom, Wenlong Xia, Zhaoyang Xu, Yashika Awasthi, Celine Geywitz et al. (+33 more)
papers.summary: Researchers found that DNA damage burden and inadequate repair contribute to selective vulnerability in neuroinflammatory injury, specifically in CUX2+ cortical neurons.

papers.title: Entanglement and electronic coherence in attosecond molecular photoionization
papers.authors: L.-M. Koll, A. J. Suñer-Rubio, T. Witting, R. Y. Bello, A. Palacios et al. (+2 more)
papers.summary: Researchers used attosecond laser pulses to study the dissociative ionization of H2 molecules. They found that entanglement between ions and photoelectrons influences electronic coherence, allowing control over this process.

papers.title: The 1000 Chinese Pangenome empowers medical and population genetics
papers.authors: Yifei Wang, Zhongqu Duan, Dan Chen, Dandan Shi, Yi Ding et al. (+16 more)
papers.summary: A team of researchers developed a pangenome-informed genome assembly workflow, enabling the creation of 1,116 diploid genome assemblies representing medically relevant genic variations.

papers.title: Gene regulatory landscape dissected by single-cell four-omics sequencing
papers.authors: Yujie Chen, Zhiyuan Liu, Heming Xu, Jiayu Liu, Mengxuan Wang et al. (+6 more)
papers.summary: Single-cell four-omics sequencing revealed the dynamics and spatial clustering of epigenome profiles across cell types and tissues, enabling sophisticated analysis of gene regulation.

papers.title: Developmental organization of sensory and sympathetic ganglia
papers.authors: Keng Ioi Vong, Yanina D. Alvarez, Qingquan Zhang, Jiaming Weng, Geoffroy Noel et al. (+16 more)
papers.summary: Researchers found that neural crest fate bias predominantly emerges within the neural tube, and only a minor subset of delaminated progenitors retain multipotency to generate both sensory and sympathetic derivatives.

papers.title: Dopaminergic mechanisms of dynamical social specialization
papers.authors: C. Solié, A. Nicolson, R. Justo, Y. Layadi, B. Morin et al. (+19 more)
papers.summary: Longitudinal tracking of mice revealed that stable, specialized social roles emerge spontaneously within groups during a foraging task, driven by dopaminergic activity in the ventral tegmental area.

papers.title: A µ-opioid receptor superagonist analgesic with minimal adverse effects
papers.authors: Juan L. Gomez, Emilya N. Ventriglia, Zachary J. Frangos, Agnieszka Sulima, Michael J. Robertson et al. (+39 more)
papers.summary: A new opioid receptor superagonist has been developed that produces analgesia with minimal adverse effects in rodent models.

papers.title: Moiré engineering of Cooper-pair density modulation states
papers.authors: Zihao Wang, Bing Xia, Stephen Paolini, Zi-Jie Yan, Pu Xiao et al. (+8 more)
papers.summary: Researchers created a moiré superlattice in Sb2Te3/FeTe bilayers, producing spatially modulated superconducting gaps that can be tuned by replacing Sb2Te3 with Bi2Te3.

papers.title: Electric dipole moment drives the dynamics of the TNFR1 complex I signalosome
papers.authors: Jianping Liu, Jing Zhao, Jiayang Gao, Kun Zhao, Yaoyao Han et al. (+13 more)
papers.summary: Long-range interactions mediated by protein electric dipole moments drive the assembly and disassembly of a complex involved in NF-κB signalling.

papers.title: Evidence of the pair-instability gap from black-hole masses
papers.authors: Hui Tong, Maya Fishbach, Eric Thrane, Matthew Mould, Thomas A. Callister et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: LIGO–Virgo–KAGRA’s fourth Gravitational-Wave Transient Catalog shows evidence of a clear pair-instability gap in the distribution of binary black-hole secondary masses.

papers.title: Expansion of outer cortical CUX2 neurons requires adaptations for DNA repair
papers.authors: Wenlong Xia, Laura Morcom, Zhaoyang Xu, I-Ling Lu, Qing Wang et al. (+15 more)
papers.summary: The transcription factor ATF4 regulates double-stranded DNA repair within vulnerable CUX2+ upper-layer 2/3 cortical neurons, enabling their survival during development.

papers.title: AhR inhibition promotes axon regeneration via a stress–growth switch
papers.authors: Dalia Halawani, Yiqun Wang, Jiaxi Li, Daniel Halperin, Haofei Ni et al. (+7 more)
papers.summary: Researchers found that inhibiting a protein called AhR promotes axon regeneration, which is the growth of nerve fibers. This discovery could lead to new treatments for nerve damage and paralysis.

papers.title: Substantial aircraft contrail formation at low soot emission levels
papers.authors: Christiane Voigt, Raphael Märkl, Daniel Sauer, Rebecca Dischl, Charles Renard et al. (+25 more)
papers.summary: Contrails, or clouds formed by airplane exhaust, can still form even when soot emissions are low. This challenges the idea that reducing soot emissions will automatically reduce contrail formation.

papers.title: Investigating the reproducibility of the social and behavioural sciences
papers.authors: Olivia Miske, Anna Lou Abatayo, Mason Daley, Mirka Dirzo, Nicholas Fox et al. (+123 more)
papers.summary: A study of over 600 papers in the social and behavioral sciences found that more recent papers are more likely to be reproducible, and that journals that require data sharing have higher reproducibility rates.

papers.title: Stoichiometric FeTe is a superconductor
papers.authors: Zi-Jie Yan, Zihao Wang, Bing Xia, Stephen Paolini, Ying-Ting Chan et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: Researchers discovered that stoichiometric FeTe, a material previously thought to be an antiferromagnetic metal, is actually a superconductor. This finding challenges our current understanding of this material.

papers.title: Investigating the replicability of the social and behavioural sciences
papers.authors: Andrew H. Tyner, Anna Lou Abatayo, Mason Daley, Samuel Field, Nicholas Fox et al. (+287 more)
papers.summary: A large-scale study found that about half of the results from social and behavioral science journals can be replicated, which challenges the idea that many studies in this field are not replicable.

papers.title: Nanoscale transfer-printed full-colour ultrahigh-resolution quantum dot LEDs
papers.authors: Lihua Lin, Jie Wang, Hailong Hu, Haolin Luo, Yanbin Liu et al. (+8 more)
papers.summary: Researchers developed a method for manufacturing high-performance full-color ultrahigh-resolution quantum dot LEDs using a dual-action force dynamics strategy.

papers.title: Angle evolution of the superconducting phase diagram in twisted bilayer WSe<sub>2</sub>
papers.authors: Yinjie Guo, John Cenker, Ammon Fischer, Daniel Muñoz-Segovia, Jordan Pack et al. (+11 more)
papers.summary: A study found that superconductivity in twisted bilayer WSe2 evolves smoothly with twist angle and emerges near Fermi surface reconstruction.

papers.title: Flexible ensheathment of axons enables myelination of complex CNS networks
papers.authors: Cody L. Call, Sarah A. Neely, Jason J. Early, Owen G. James, Lida Zoupi et al. (+6 more)
papers.summary: Researchers discovered that axons can be ensheathed to enable myelination of complex CNS networks, which is crucial for neural function.

papers.title: Evolution of pandemic cholera at its global source
papers.authors: Amber Barton, Mokibul Hassan Afrad, Alyce Taylor-Brown, Nisha Singh, Chetan Thakur et al. (+9 more)
papers.summary: A study found that the seventh cholera pandemic evolved separately in Bangladesh and India, with the Ganges Basin acting as the global source.

papers.title: Investigating the analytical robustness of the social and behavioural sciences
papers.authors: Balazs Aczel, Barnabas Szaszi, Harry T. Clelland, Marton Kovacs, Felix Holzmeister et al. (+489 more)
papers.summary: An investigation found that social and behavioural science claims have limited robustness, with only 34% of reanalyses closely matching the original results.

papers.title: Dual-symmetry-guided assembly of complex lattices
papers.authors: Huang Fang, Xiaotian Li, Wensi Sun, Chengxin Wang, Nuo Chen et al. (+5 more)
papers.summary: A dual-symmetry-guided strategy has been developed for assembling complex lattices and quasicrystalline structures. This method provides a general and experimentally accessible route to complex-symmetry materials.

papers.title: A chelicera-bearing arthropod reveals the Cambrian origin of chelicerates
papers.authors: Rudy Lerosey-Aubril, Javier Ortega-Hernández
papers.summary: A new arthropod species, Megachelicerax cousteaui, has been discovered in the Cambrian period. It features massive chelicerae and provides evidence for the origin of chelicerates.

papers.title: An enteric neuron ionotropic receptor regulates salt stress resistance
papers.authors: Jihye Yeon, Jinmahn Kim, Koji Sato, Stephen Nurrish, Laurie Chen et al. (+8 more)
papers.summary: A specific enteric neuron in Caenorhabditis elegans has been found to regulate salt stress resistance. The GLR-9 ionotropic receptor is responsible for detecting high-salt conditions.

papers.title: Deconstruction of a spino-brain–spinal cord circuit that drives chronic pain
papers.authors: Qian Wang, Joo Han Lee, Gregory Nachtrab, Yuan Yuan, Lei Yuan et al. (+5 more)
papers.summary: A circuit between the spinal cord and brain has been identified as a key driver of chronic pain in mice. The circuit involves spinal-cord-projecting neurons in the rostral ventromedial medulla.

papers.title: General scales unlock AI evaluation with explanatory and predictive power
papers.authors: Lexin Zhou, Lorenzo Pacchiardi, Fernando Martínez-Plumed, Katherine M. Collins, Yael Moros-Daval et al. (+21 more)
papers.summary: A new methodology has been developed for evaluating the capabilities of AI systems. The approach uses rubrics to capture cognitive and intellectual demands, providing explanatory and predictive power.

papers.title: Hydroxy-induced cobalt oxides for syngas to light olefins
papers.authors: Yu Han, Jiafeng Yu, Jian Wei, Chuanyan Fang, Jianxiang Han et al. (+7 more)
papers.summary: A new catalyst made from cobalt oxides and hydroxy promoters improves syngas to light olefins conversion using Fischer–Tropsch synthesis.

papers.title: Active dissociation of intracortical spiking and high gamma activity
papers.authors: Tianhao Lei, Michael R. Scheid, Robert D. Flint, Joshua I. Glaser, Marc W. Slutzky
papers.summary: A brain–machine interface study in monkeys reveals the biophysical underpinnings of cortical high gamma-band activity.

papers.title: Reproducibility and robustness of economics and political science research
papers.authors: Abel Brodeur, Derek Mikola, Nikolai Cook, Lenka Fiala, Thomas Brailey et al. (+342 more)
papers.summary: A study of economics and political science research finds high levels of robustness and reproducibility, despite data availability and author characteristics.

papers.title: Catalyst diverts course of industrial process to make valuable hydrocarbons
papers.authors: Jingxiu Xie
papers.summary: A new catalyst with two cobalt components could lead to sustainable processes for manufacturing light olefins.

papers.title: Lift off! Artemis II mission sends humans to the Moon — opening a new era of exploration
papers.authors: Alexandra Witze
papers.summary: The Artemis II mission sends humans to the Moon, marking a new era of exploration.

papers.title: Super-potent opioids could be safer-than-expected alternatives to conventional painkillers
papers.authors: Victor Mathis, Emmanuel Darcq
papers.summary: Researchers found that modified nitazenes, a type of opioid 1,000 times stronger than morphine, had remarkably few adverse effects in rodents. This could potentially make them safer alternatives to conventional painkillers.

papers.title: Is social media addictive? Why a formal diagnosis is still out of reach
papers.authors: Dar Meshi
papers.summary: Due to limited methods and a lack of guidance around diagnostic criteria, scientists are reluctant to use the term addiction when referring to social media.

papers.title: Struggling to focus on research when the world is ‘on fire’? Some ways to cope
papers.authors: Diana Kwon
papers.summary: Researchers are struggling with the impact of distressing news on their mental health and scientific work.

papers.title: Hallucinated citations are polluting the scientific literature. What can be done?
papers.authors: Miryam Naddaf, Elizabeth Quill
papers.summary: Tens of thousands of publications from 2025 may include invalid references generated by AI, according to a Nature analysis.

papers.title: Daily briefing: The countdown to NASA’s Artemis II Moon mission launch
papers.authors: Flora Graham
papers.summary: This article provides an update on NASA's Artemis II Moon mission, including the science it will conduct and its significance.

papers.title: Cutting aircraft soot emissions is not enough to curb contrail clouds
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Contrail clouds formed by jet engines contribute to global warming. However, in-flight measurements show that contrails are formed through different mechanisms depending on soot-emission levels and fuel composition.

papers.summary: A massive project to replicate social-science studies found that half of them failed the replication test. However, this also raised hope for solutions to improve the credibility of science.

papers.title: ‘Replication games’ test the robustness of social-science studies
papers.authors: Unknown authors

papers.title: More self-reflection in research can lead to better science
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A package of papers highlights the importance of self-reflection in research to improve the quality and reliability of scientific findings.

papers.title: Why science has a credibility problem — and how to address it
papers.authors: Nicola Jones
papers.summary: A meta-research project examined the reproducibility and replicability of published results in social sciences, highlighting lessons learned from a large-scale dissection.

papers.title: 100 years of synthetic fuels

papers.summary: The Fischer–Tropsch synthesis, a method for making liquid fuels from coal, has been revisited as a route to sustainable fuels.

papers.authors: Pengliang Chi, Xiang Wang, Jialu Li, Jingrui Huang, Sicheng Ju et al. (+13 more)

papers.summary: The paper argues that carbon capture and storage is not a standard mitigation strategy for climate change, but rather a complex issue.

papers.title: Bunny or bug?
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: This paper appears to be an article from the Nature archive, discussing a curious species of rabbit and bullfinches learning to eat primroses.

papers.title: A solar system is born
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Astronomers have discovered a second planet forming from the material orbiting a young star, only the second such example known.

papers.summary: The authors argue that a responsible authorship culture is needed, and it is a collective responsibility.

papers.title: AI used in warfare needs a strong ethical framework

papers.title: The hidden costs of ‘helpful’ AI

papers.summary: Even when AI tools aid decision-making, they can quietly de-skill whole professions by narrowing how uncertainties and values are debated.

papers.title: Artemis II mission is about to fly humans to the Moon — here’s the science they’ll do

papers.summary: The NASA Artemis II mission will take astronauts around the Moon for the first time in over 50 years, marking a significant step towards lunar exploration.

papers.title: Daily briefing: Are boys really in crisis? What the science says

papers.summary: Concerns about boys are widespread, but evidence suggests that worries should be understood in the context of all young people.

papers.summary: A model-agnostic framework measures uncertainty in protein embeddings and assesses their biological relevance to improve model reliability.

papers.summary: A new method called ROCKET improves experimental structure determination by combining information from a pre-trained neural network with X-ray crystallography and cryo-electron microscopy experiments.

papers.title: Two-dimensional materials for integrated sensing
papers.authors: Hangyu Xu, Zhihao Xu, Qinqi Ren, Yuan Meng, Sangmoon Han et al. (+4 more)
papers.summary: Two-dimensional materials have potential for in situ encoding and optoelectronic sensing in computing technologies, but challenges remain for integrated sensing.

papers.title: Bulk nano-heterointerface secures molecular contacts in perovskite solar cells
papers.authors: Yixin Luo, Jiahui Shen, Ke Zhao, Shenglong Chu, Yuan Tian et al. (+25 more)
papers.summary: A bulk nano-heterointerface design improves the operational stability of perovskite solar cells by strengthening molecular contacts.

papers.title: Hydrogels find their inner magnetism
papers.authors: Stephen J. Blundell
papers.summary: Hydrogels containing C=O groups and calcium cations exhibit an unexpected paramagnetic effect with potential biomedical applications.

papers.title: Nanophotonic chip-space interfaces for multidimensional nonlinear optics
papers.authors: Dunzhao Wei, Bo Chen, Shuai Wan, Yixuan Wang, Jiantao Ma et al. (+13 more)
papers.summary: A nanophotonic chip-space interface generates on-demand nonlinear structured light, including tunable optical vortices and spatiotemporal vortex pulses.

papers.title: Static magnetization switching in an artificial antiferromagnetic multilayer driven by a voltage-controlled magnetic anisotropy effect
papers.authors: Hiroyasu Nakayama, Takayuki Nozaki, Toshiki Yamaji, Tomohiro Nozaki, Hiroshi Imamura et al. (+1 more)
papers.summary: Researchers demonstrated static magnetization switching using voltage-controlled magnetic anisotropy without pulse width sensitivity.

papers.title: Removing aluminium impurities in primary magnesium at an ultra-low cost
papers.authors: Rui Zheng, Bo Yang, Yue-Cun Wang, Bo-Yu Liu, Wei-Yi Yang et al. (+1 more)
papers.summary: A cost-effective method to remove aluminum impurities from primary magnesium was developed, enabling large-scale production of high-purity magnesium.

papers.title: Option for transparent peer review
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: Nature Materials introduced an option for transparent peer review, allowing authors to include reviewer comments and rebuttal letters with their publication.

papers.title: Tuning phonon transmission via single-atom substituents

papers.summary: Atomic-level changes to molecular junctions were found to significantly affect phonon transmission, with single atoms influencing thermal conductance.

papers.title: A toolkit for modeling cis-regulatory logic of enhancers at large scale
papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A software package called CREsted was developed to model and analyze enhancer regulatory logic at a large scale, using single-cell chromatin accessibility data.

papers.title: CREsted: modeling genomic and synthetic cell-type-specific enhancers across tissues and species
papers.authors: Niklas Kempynck, Seppe De Winter, Casper H. Blaauw, Vasileios Konstantakos, Eren Can Ekşi et al. (+10 more)
papers.summary: CREsted is a toolbox for analyzing, modeling, and designing cell-type-specific enhancers across various species. It's efficient and user-friendly.

papers.title: Synaptic connectomics by sequencing

papers.summary: A new sequencing-based method maps synaptic connections by capturing paired neuronal barcodes from intact fragments, enabling comprehensive brain connectivity maps.

papers.authors: Unknown authors
papers.summary: A model-agnostic measure quantifies the reliability of language model-inferred protein, gene, and genome representations.

papers.title: An instrument named Cannoli

papers.title: Quantifying uncertainty in protein representations across models and tasks

papers.summary: A model-agnostic framework measures uncertainty in protein embeddings and assesses their biological relevance to improve model reliability.

papers.title: AlphaFold as a prior: experimental structure determination conditioned on a pretrained neural network
papers.authors: Alisia Fadini, Minhuan Li, Airlie J. McCoy, Suresh Banjara, Hiroki Okumura et al. (+8 more)
